method_atom: an exact treatment of a many-body system's response to a sudden change of a control parameter (a quench), using a self-consistent mean-field approximation that becomes exact in a high-dimensional limit, to follow the non-equilibrium relaxation toward a non-thermal steady state on a timescale set by the inverse of the coupling energy scale, with transient collapse-and-revival oscillations at a period set by the perturbation strength, and characterizing the final state by a maximum-entropy ensemble constrained by an extensive set of conserved quantities
object_atom: a lattice model of itinerant electrons interacting locally with a species of immobile particles (the Falicov-Kimball model), driven across the boundary between its metallic and insulating phases by an abrupt change of the electron interaction strength
brief_reason: the quench-plus-mean-field dynamics, the coupling-set relaxation and revival timescales, and the constrained-maximum-entropy (generalized Gibbs) description are transferable machinery, while the electrons-and-frozen-particles lattice model with its metal-insulator transition is the condensed-matter-specific object.
